title: christiana eburnea
side_effects: Christiana eburnea can have side effects if used improperly, such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches, which can be caused by an overdose of its active compounds. In severe cases, prolonged use of the plant can lead to kidney damage, liver inflammation, and even respiratory failure. Additionally, improper use of Christiana eburnea can also cause allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and swelling, particularly in individuals with pre-existing sensitivities. Furthermore, the plant's toxic compounds can interact with certain medications, exacerbating existing health conditions or causing new ones to develop.
